briefings.2026-06-30: A handful of stories highlight ongoing facility changes across German-speaking Europe and Belgium. In Germany, a new climbing gym is taking shape in Cologne-Deutz while the former Exxpozed Kletterhalle in the Allgäu region has closed, with the space being taken over by a traditional clothing retailer. In Austria, plans to convert a former Innsbruck church into a climbing hall were discussed at a circular-building industry networking event. Meanwhile in Belgium, the Face Nord climbing gym in Mons is embroiled in a legal dispute with the contractor responsible for its construction works.

stories.title: French gym tests inclusive 'vertical walking' device for overweight and senior climbers
stories.summary: A new accessibility device called 'Footing vertical' is being trialled at the Climb Up gym in Ponts-de-Cé (Maine-et-Loire), designed to make climbing more accessible to overweight people and seniors. The invention is described as revolutionary and is expected to become more widely available soon.

stories.title: Austrian hospital staff petition for new climbing wall in Osttirol
stories.summary: Around a third of staff at the Lienz hospital (BKH Lienz) in Osttirol, Austria, are reportedly pushing for a new climbing hall to be built in the region via a petition. Local media report ongoing debate over whether the area needs dedicated climbing gym infrastructure.

stories.title: Private climbing gym planned for Le Locle, Switzerland
stories.summary: A new privately funded climbing gym is set to open at the Communal site in Le Locle, Switzerland, with construction costs estimated at 1.3 million Swiss francs. The facility is expected to be completed by Christmas 2026.

stories.title: Austrian funding boosts climbing course and bouldering wall expansion in Steyr
stories.summary: The Naturfreunde Steyr climbing organisation has received €32,000 in public funding to expand the course area and bouldering wall at its climbing hall. The grant covers eligible construction costs for the facility upgrade.

stories.title: German climbing hall in Kressbronn adopts digital route-setting system
stories.summary: The TV Kressbronn climbing hall on Lake Constance, Germany, has introduced a digital route-setting system as part of a modernisation push. The move marks a step toward technology-driven route management at the facility.

stories.title: Labor dispute at French Climb Up chain highlights commercial shift in climbing gyms
stories.summary: Staff at a Climb Up climbing gym in Ponts-de-Cé, France, have gone on strike amid a broader social conflict reflecting the chain's increasingly business-driven approach. The dispute, covered by Libération, centres on cost-cutting measures eliminating less profitable services.

stories.title: German churches being converted into climbing gyms amid declining congregations
stories.summary: As church membership continues to fall in Germany, some disused church buildings are being repurposed as climbing gyms, according to a report by FR. The trend reflects creative reuse of religious real estate for new sport and leisure purposes.

stories.title: New bouldering hall nears completion in Burgdorf, Switzerland after seven-year wait
stories.summary: A long-planned bouldering hall in Burgdorf, Switzerland, first proposed by a local association in 2019, is expected to open in summer 2026. The project has taken seven years to come to fruition.

stories.title: New climbing gym 'Goat Rocks' opens near Nancy, France
stories.summary: A new climbing gym called Goat Rocks has opened in Messein, near Nancy, France, billed as a 'new generation' climbing facility. Local media outlet France Bleu Sud Lorraine featured a review of the new venue.

stories.title: Mons Climbing Gym Face Nord in Legal Dispute Over Construction Works
stories.summary: The Face Nord climbing gym in Mons, Belgium, is in litigation with the company responsible for its construction site. Details of the dispute were reported via a regional news outlet's social media post.

stories.title: New Climbing Gym Taking Shape in Cologne-Deutz
stories.summary: A new climbing hall is being built inside a brown box-shaped building in Cologne's Deutz district, near the trade fair tower. The Kölner Stadt-Anzeiger reports on the project's progress as construction continues.

stories.title: Former Exxpozed Climbing Gym in Allgäu Closes, Replaced by Traditional Clothing Retailer
stories.summary: The Exxpozed climbing gym in Dietmannsried, in Germany's Allgäu region, has shut down. A traditional Bavarian clothing ('Tracht') retailer is now moving into the space with ambitious expansion plans, including a logistics centre.

stories.title: Innsbruck Church to Be Converted into Climbing Hall
stories.summary: A circular building industry networking event in Austria highlighted plans to repurpose a former Innsbruck church as a climbing gym. The conversion was presented as an example of adaptive reuse within the country's circular construction sector.

stories.title: New competition wall unveiled at Chambéry Escalade climbing club
stories.summary: The Chambéry Escalade climbing club has reopened its facility featuring a new competition wall. The renovation supports the club's training and competitive climbing programmes.

stories.title: Largest climbing gym in the Baltic states to open in Vilnius
stories.summary: Vilnius will soon be home to the largest climbing gym in the Baltic states, with a grand opening ceremony planned for April 2. The new facility will offer climbers access to all areas for the first time during the launch event.

stories.title: New leisure complex with climbing gym completes Val-d'Oise economic zone
stories.summary: A new leisure park including a bowling alley, trampoline park, climbing gym, and laser game venue is set to open in the Bois Rochefort economic zone in Cormeilles-en-Parisis, Val-d'Oise. The development marks the completion of this commercial district near Paris.

stories.title: Austrian Alpine Club climbing hall to close for maintenance
stories.summary: The Alpenverein's Kletterhalle Yspertal will close for scheduled maintenance and route-setting work from June 18 to July 5, 2026. The facility will be inaccessible to climbers during this period.

stories.title: Potential buyer eyes former church in Herford for climbing gym conversion
stories.summary: As the Christuskirche in Herford, Germany seeks a new owner, local officials have indicated openness to a buyer converting the building into a climbing gym. A dedication event for the church's future use is planned for July 11.

stories.title: €50 million Rotterdam leisure park with climbing wall may never open
stories.summary: A 25,000 square metre indoor leisure park in Rotterdam, built at a cost of €50 million and featuring a rollercoaster, ice rink, escape room, and climbing gym, stands completely empty and may never open to the public. The financial troubles highlight risks in large-scale multi-attraction leisure developments that include climbing facilities.

stories.title: L'Orangerie opens in Grenoble combining climbing gym, restaurant and bakery
stories.summary: A new venue called L'Orangerie has opened in Grenoble, France, combining a climbing gym with a restaurant and bakery. The space includes an outdoor terrace that is planned to be expanded further.

stories.title: Bad Tölz Budget Report: Town Finances Stable, Climbing Hall Renovation Continues
stories.summary: A municipal budget report for Bad Tölz confirms the town's finances remain stable, with ongoing projects including the renovation of the local climbing hall (Kletterhalle) set to be completed alongside street lighting modernisation. The renovation is part of a broader set of public infrastructure projects funded under the current budget.

stories.title: Repeated Break-Ins at Liège Basilica Slated for Future Climbing Gym
stories.summary: Police in Liège have intervened nine times in under three months at the Basilique de Cointe, addressing break-ins and theft at the currently unoccupied building. The site is planned to house a climbing gym, complicating security and renovation plans ahead of its intended opening.

stories.title: Le Triangle: Paris's First Fully Independent City-Centre Climbing Gym
stories.summary: Le Triangle in Bercy Village is highlighted as Paris's first fully independent climbing gym located in the city centre, offering 900 square metres dedicated entirely to climbing. The gym is positioned as a notable addition to the capital's growing indoor climbing scene.

stories.title: Luxembourg's ClimbX Gym Remains Closed Following December Accident, Municipality Promises Stricter Rules
stories.summary: The ClimbX climbing gym in Lallingen, Luxembourg, has remained closed since an accident in December 2025, prompting opposition councillors in Esch to demand answers from local authorities. The municipality has announced it will introduce stricter climbing safety regulations in response.

stories.title: Entrepreneurs Convert Church into Modern Climbing Gym
stories.summary: Two entrepreneurs, including Marc Ihl, have transformed a former church into a modern climbing and bouldering gym. The project reflects a wider trend of repurposing disused German churches for leisure and sport use.

stories.title: Solothurn Bouldering Gym Bimano Under Financial Pressure
stories.summary: The Bimano Boulderhalle in Solothurn, Switzerland, founded by Jasmin Blaser along with Jano Böhlen and Wolfgang Antz, is reportedly facing significant financial difficulties. The report raises concerns about the gym's continued operation.

stories.title: Hannover Zoo's Indoor Climbing Hall Faces Questions Over Viability
stories.summary: The Rotunde building at Hannover Zoo, which now houses an indoor climbing hall, previously hosted failed attractions including a 360-degree jungle panorama and photo exhibitions. The piece examines whether the climbing concept can succeed where prior uses struggled.

stories.title: Leutesdorf Church Considered for Climbing Hall Conversion
stories.summary: A regional association is exploring a new usage concept for the Wallfahrtskirche Heilig Kreuz in Leutesdorf, Germany, with conversion into a climbing hall among the options being discussed. The plan would form part of broader efforts to repurpose the pilgrimage church sustainably.

stories.title: Liège Basilica Among Sites Considered for Climbing Gym Conversion
stories.summary: As part of a wider initiative to find new uses for around 500 churches in Liège, Belgium, the Basilique de Cointe and adjoining memorial site have been proposed as a potential climbing gym conversion. Organiser Isabelle Rihon is running brainstorming sessions to generate ideas for repurposing the city's religious buildings.

stories.title: Climb Up opens 31st French climbing gym in Le Mans
stories.summary: Lyon-based chain Climb Up has opened a new climbing gym in Le Mans, its 31st location in France. The opening is part of the operator's continued expansion across French cities.

stories.title: Bouldering gym The Roof in Poitiers adds new climbing area
stories.summary: The Roof, a bouldering gym in Poitiers, has inaugurated a new climbing space adding 30 linear metres of additional climbing surface. The expansion increases the gym's overall capacity for climbers.

stories.title: Vertical Park du Pontet celebrates first year of strong growth
stories.summary: The Vertical Park climbing gym in Le Pontet is marking its first anniversary with reported strong business success. Founders Nicolas Bastide and Maxime Vegas built the venue as more than a simple climbing hall, incorporating broader leisure offerings.

stories.title: €10.5 million climbing centre in Ravensburg hangs in the balance
stories.summary: A planned XXL climbing centre costing €10.5 million in Ravensburg, Germany, faces a critical decision in May on whether the project will proceed. The German Alpine Club (DAV) section in Ravensburg is appealing to members ahead of the decisive vote.

stories.title: Climb Up Brest introduces vertical accessibility system for elderly and disabled climbers
stories.summary: Since January, the Climb Up climbing gym in Brest has offered a 'vertical footing' system enabling elderly people and those with disabilities to try climbing. The innovation is designed to broaden access to indoor climbing for underserved groups.

stories.title: Esch unveils new operating rules for ClimbX climbing wall after accident
stories.summary: Following an accident at the ClimbX climbing wall, the city of Esch-sur-Alzette in Luxembourg has presented revised operating regulations. The new rules will overhaul usage protocols and clarify responsibility at the facility.

stories.title: Mons climbing gym Face Nord fails crowdfunding goal, project delayed
stories.summary: The Face Nord climbing gym project in Mons, Belgium failed to reach its crowdfunding target and must postpone its plans. Organisers expressed disappointment at the lack of community support for the campaign, which closed on 19 April.

stories.title: Karma La Villette federal climbing gym officially inaugurated in Paris
stories.summary: After opening to the public in January, the FFME federal climbing gym Karma La Villette in Paris was officially inaugurated on 10 April. The venue represents an expansion of the French Mountaineering and Climbing Federation's gym network.

stories.title: Baltic Rocks in Schwerin to expand boulder hall by summer 2026
stories.summary: Climbing gym Baltic Rocks in Schwerin, Germany, is expanding its bouldering hall, with completion expected by summer 2026. The facility already offers interactive climbing features, Ninja Warrior elements, and skill games alongside traditional bouldering.

stories.title: Lorient leisure complex with climbing wall and padel courts delayed by legal challenge
stories.summary: A planned leisure complex near a Decathlon store in Lorient, which now includes a climbing wall, padel courts and a restaurant (golf and Pilates concepts have been dropped), has been delayed due to a legal appeal. The project's scope has been revised to focus on these three core activities.

stories.title: Rennes redevelopment: climbing gym 'The Roof' opens at historic Saint-Georges site
stories.summary: As part of the redevelopment of the Palais Saint-Georges and Saint-Melaine area in Rennes, a new climbing gym called 'The Roof' has opened alongside a restaurant, with a coworking space and further projects planned for the site.

stories.title: Chambéry climbing and trampoline halls running at full capacity amid wet weather
stories.summary: Indoor climbing and trampoline facilities in the Chambéry area are reportedly packed during periods of rainy weather, as families seek indoor activity options. Operators note the need to find sufficient activities to meet demand on wet days.

stories.title: Climbing gym proposed as future option for Heilig Kreuz site in Germany
stories.summary: Local planners are considering a climbing gym as a way to fill a leisure gap at the Heilig Kreuz site, potentially in cooperation with a nearby youth hostel, to boost local and tourist appeal.

stories.title: New large climbing gym under construction in northern Germany
stories.summary: A regional news report referencing local elections in Schwerin also notes that a company is building what is described as the largest climbing gym in northern Germany, though further details on location and opening date were not specified in the source.

stories.title: XXL Climbing Gym Altissimo Inaugurated in Saint-Geours-de-Maremne
stories.summary: The Altissimo climbing gym, a large-format facility in Saint-Geours-de-Maremne (Landes), was officially inaugurated on Saturday 25 April 2026. The opening adds a major new bouldering and lead climbing venue to southwest France.

stories.title: Hybrid Café-and-Climbing Venue 'Bon Matin' Opens in Etterbeek, Brussels
stories.summary: Bon Matin, a new venue housed in a former industrial warehouse in Etterbeek, combines a neighbourhood café with a climbing gym under one roof. The hybrid concept lets visitors climb and then socialise over coffee in the same space.

stories.title: New Community Bouldering Space Launches in Genval, Belgium
stories.summary: A citizen-led project called 'Entre Ciel et Terre' has launched a community space built around a bouldering gym in Genval, billed as a first of its kind in Belgium. The initiative is run as a non-profit citizen association.

stories.title: Upzone 360 Climbing Gym Opens in Rodez, France
stories.summary: A new climbing gym, Upzone 360, opened on 25 April at 19 Avenue Amans Rodat in Rodez. Founder Sébastien Anglade designed the venue as an all-public space featuring climbing walls alongside a dedicated multi-use area.

stories.title: Lienz Tourism Board Revives Talk of Building a Climbing Hall
stories.summary: Franz Theurl, head of the Lienz tourism association (Alpinplattform Lienz), raised the long-discussed proposal for a local climbing hall at the body's general assembly. The newly elected board signalled openness to reviving the stalled project.

stories.title: New Family Climbing Gym with 'Fun Walls' Opens in Paris's Montparnasse
stories.summary: A new climbing gym featuring playful 'fun walls' aimed at families has opened in the Montparnasse district of Paris, according to a roundup of May 2026 sports activities in the Île-de-France region.

stories.title: Former Church in Bad Orb Converted into 'Boulder Church' Climbing Gym
stories.summary: A disused church in Bad Orb, Hesse, has been transformed into a bouldering gym named 'Boulder Church.' The conversion was a technical challenge, and operators worked closely with the diocese to avoid an invasive renovation.

stories.title: Bloc Session Climbing Gym in Salon-de-Provence Celebrates First Anniversary
stories.summary: Bloc Session in Salon-de-Provence has become a popular fixture in the local community after one year of operation. The gym is marking its first anniversary, according to local newspaper La Provence.

stories.title: Würzburg's St. Andreas Church to Be Converted into a Climbing Gym
stories.summary: The Diocese of Würzburg has approved turning the Catholic St. Andreas church into a climbing gym, resolving a prior legal dispute for the operators. The move reflects a wider trend of repurposing redundant church buildings for sports use in Germany.

stories.title: Würzburg Church to Become Bavaria's First 'Climbing Church'
stories.summary: Rock Inn, operator of a Würzburg bouldering gym, plans to convert the deconsecrated St. Andreas church into a climbing and bouldering hall over the next year. The project would mark Bavaria's first conversion of a church into a climbing facility, repurposing the former worship space for climbing, bouldering and yoga.

stories.title: UK Indoor Climbing Market Faces Consolidation Amid Rising Costs
stories.summary: French industry outlet Vertige Media reports that the UK indoor climbing sector is undergoing consolidation as operators face declining footfall, rising costs and increasingly complex business operations. The piece notes that running a climbing gym, particularly in London, now requires sophisticated management as the market matures beyond its early growth phase.

stories.title: Climbing Club to Build New Gym in Jona, Open to Non-Members
stories.summary: The Boulderverein Rapperswil-Jona is building a new climbing gym and plans to make it accessible to non-members as well as club members. The facility marks an expansion of climbing access in the Swiss canton of St. Gallen.

stories.title: Disused Churches in Bavaria Being Converted into Climbing Gyms
stories.summary: Several churches in Germany, including one in Würzburg, are being repurposed as climbing gyms as congregations shrink and buildings need new uses. A church in Saint-André could become the first in Bavaria converted into a climbing facility, reflecting a wider European trend of adaptive reuse for declining religious buildings.

stories.title: New Bouldering Gym 'Upzone 360' Opens in Rodez City Centre
stories.summary: A new bouldering gym called Upzone 360 has opened in the centre of Rodez, France, offering an accessible and evolving climbing experience for local enthusiasts. The gym is open seven days a week.

stories.title: New Climbing Gym 'Bloc à Bleau' Planned Near Fontainebleau with Bar Concept
stories.summary: Three project leaders from Samoreau are set to open a new climbing gym called Bloc à Bleau near Fontainebleau, featuring an attached bar and a 'living space' concept. The founders will manage both the climbing facility and the bar themselves.

stories.title: Old Boiler House to Become Modern Climbing Gym in Germany
stories.summary: A €6 million conversion project will transform a former boiler house (Kesselhaus) into a modern climbing gym, according to local officials. The redevelopment has already received planning approval as part of wider town development plans.

stories.title: Vacant XXL Hall in Mühlhausen Could Become Climbing Gym, Petrol Station, or Other Use
stories.summary: Following an insolvency, the large XXL-Halle property in Mühlhausen, Thuringia is seeking a new tenant, with options including a climbing gym, paintball arena, mini-golf course, or a petrol station. No final decision has been made on the building's future use.

stories.title: German Alpine Club Shelves New Climbing Gym Plans in Frankenthal, Opts for Renovation
stories.summary: The Deutscher Alpenverein's Frankenthal section has paused its plans to build a new climbing gym, citing frustration over stalled progress, and will instead focus on renovating its existing facility for now. The new-build project remains in limbo pending further resolution.

stories.title: XXL Climbing Gym Altissimo Inaugurated in Saint-Geours-de-Maremne
stories.summary: The XXL climbing gym Altissimo in Saint-Geours-de-Maremne, in the Landes region of France, was officially inaugurated on 25 April 2026 in the presence of local elected officials. Attendees described the facility as exceptionally large for the area.

stories.title: Paris's Largest Bouldering Gym 'Climb World' Hiring Ahead of Opening
stories.summary: A job listing for a full-time, multi-purpose front-of-house role in Paris's 14th arrondissement confirms the upcoming opening of Climb World, described as the largest bouldering gym in Paris. The recruitment drive signals the gym's launch is imminent.

stories.title: Climbing Gym 'Kraftreaktor' Begins Construction on Planned Extension
stories.summary: The Kraftreaktor climbing gym in Lenzburg, Switzerland has started building work on a long-planned extension. Owners Franziska and Marco Toretti, who took over the gym in 2018, say the project is proceeding on schedule.

stories.title: Brussels Climbing Gym Cancels European Youth Competition
stories.summary: Le Camp de Base, a 1,600m² climbing gym in Ixelles, Brussels, has cancelled its hosting of the World Climbing Europe Youth Series. In a lengthy statement, gym management said the decision was not directed against any specific competitors but reflected the venue's responsibility as employer and site owner to determine under what conditions events can be held.

stories.title: Opening of New Climbing Gym in Völs, Austria Delayed
stories.summary: A new climbing gym in Völs, Austria, which was expected to open within months, has seen construction work fail to begin on schedule. Local media outline the reasons behind the ongoing delay.

stories.title: Former Industrial Building in Ilmenau to Become Modern Climbing Gym
stories.summary: Plans to convert the historic Fischerhütte industrial building in Ilmenau, Germany into a modern climbing gym are becoming more concrete. The project, first reported last year, is now moving toward implementation.

stories.title: Debate Over Future of Marienkirche: Climbing Gym, Museum, or Something Else?
stories.summary: Local authorities in Rheda-Wiedenbrück, Germany are debating possible future uses for the historic Marienkirche, with a climbing gym among the options being considered alongside a museum.

stories.title: Climbing Gym Hosts Apprenticeship Speed-Dating Event for Hamburg Youth
stories.summary: The Nordwandhalle climbing gym in Hamburg hosted an apprenticeship 'speed-dating' event, drawing around 400 visitors seeking information on vocational training and career paths.

stories.title: Swiss Canton Approves Funding for Climbing Hall in Näfels
stories.summary: The cantonal government of Glarus, Switzerland has approved funding contributions for sports facility projects, including the climbing hall in Näfels alongside the Buchholz athletics facility.

stories.title: New Climbing Gym Opens in Belgium Following Fac Trophy 2026
stories.summary: A newly built climbing gym was highlighted as a special feature of the Fac Trophy 2026 event in Belgium, with the freshly renovated venue now ready to welcome climbers.

stories.title: Edelrid Issues Urgent Safety Warning for OHM Belay Devices
stories.summary: Edelrid has issued an urgent safety notice for its OHM II, OHMEGA and FUSE assisted-braking devices after a defective unit was discovered in a climbing gym in April 2026, where the sleeve and screw were found to be made from incorrect materials. Climbing gyms across Europe using these devices are advised to check their equipment.

stories.title: New Climbing Club Launches in Pontivy, Brittany
stories.summary: A year after the opening of the Wallness climbing gym in Pontivy (Morbihan), founders Ghislain Guidrais and his partner Laëtitia have launched a new climbing club to grow the sport in central Brittany. The initiative aims to build on the gym's first year of operation by fostering a local climbing community.

stories.title: Mühlhausen Debates Climbing Hall vs Petrol Station for New 'XXL' Leisure Site
stories.summary: Local authorities in Mühlhausen, Germany are debating options for a large new development site, with an indoor climbing hall among the proposed uses alongside an indoor playground, minigolf, and paintball. However, a development plan dating back more than 30 years restricts what can actually be built.

stories.title: Austrian Gyms Unite for 10-Hour Charity Climbing Marathon
stories.summary: The ClimbingZoo association and Kletterhalle Kraftreich organised a 10-hour charity climbing event spanning 11 climbing gyms and involving 130 climbers in support of a good cause. Organisers thanked participants, sponsors, and host venues for their involvement.

stories.title: Canton Glarus Considers Further Funding for Näfels Climbing Hall Expansion
stories.summary: The Swiss canton of Glarus is reviewing contributions to local sports facilities, including the climbing hall in Näfels, which has been regularly expanded and renovated since opening in 2004. The Verein Kletteranlagen Linthgebiet (VKL) is seeking continued support for further upgrades.

stories.title: French Climbing Instructor Opens Up on €1,400 Monthly Salary
stories.summary: A French climbing instructor and route-setter, Lucas, has shared details of his finances, revealing he earns around €1,400 per month working at a large commercial climbing gym. The piece highlights wider concerns about pay levels for climbing gym staff in France.

stories.title: Liechtenstein Climbing Hall Construction Fuels Parking Dispute
stories.summary: A new climbing hall under construction near a public swimming pool in Liechtenstein is taking up additional space, contributing to a reduction in available parking and sparking local debate. Residents and officials are discussing how to balance the new facility's footprint with existing parking demand.

stories.title: Pontivy Gym Owners Launch New Climbing Club
stories.summary: Ghislain and Laëtitia Guidrais, operators of the Wallness climbing gym in Pontivy, France, have launched their own climbing club and school to make the sport more accessible to locals. The initiative marks an expansion of the gym's offering beyond standard facility access.

stories.title: Austrian Climbing Federation Takeover of Graz Hall Raises Concerns
stories.summary: The Austrian Alpine Club has voiced concerns that a planned takeover of Graz's only major climbing hall by the Austrian Climbing Federation could disadvantage recreational and grassroots climbers. Critics argue the city already lags behind in climbing infrastructure, and the change in ownership structure may worsen access for the broader public.

stories.title: DAV Nürnberg Updates Members on Climbing Factory Lease Talks
stories.summary: At its April 2026 members' meeting, DAV Nürnberg's section decided to enter negotiations with the landlord of its Climbing Factory facility. The update outlines the current status of discussions concerning the hall's future tenancy arrangements.

stories.title: Climb World, Paris's Largest Bouldering Gym, Opens in Montparnasse
stories.summary: Climb World has opened at the Ateliers Gaîté development in Paris's 14th arrondissement, billed as the largest bouldering gym in the city at 2,800 square metres. The new facility adds significant capacity to the Paris climbing gym market.

stories.title: Investor Sawiris to build mega sports centre with climbing hall in Andermatt
stories.summary: Egyptian investor Samih Sawiris is funding a large new sports complex in Andermatt, Switzerland, featuring a swimming pool, spa, ice rink and climbing hall. The multi-million franc project adds to Sawiris's broader development push in the Andermatt resort area.

stories.title: Biberach climbing centre sees membership numbers explode at five-year mark
stories.summary: The Kletterzentrum Biberach is celebrating its fifth anniversary with strong growth, with the German Alpine Club (DAV) reporting membership rising from 3,400 to 5,700 since opening. Local DAV chairman Klaus Hassler called the facility a proven success.

stories.title: Paris's largest bouldering gym opens in the 14th arrondissement
stories.summary: Climb World, billed as the largest bouldering facility in Paris, has opened on the lower level of Les Ateliers Gaîté in the 14th arrondissement. The new gym adds significant bouldering capacity to the capital's climbing scene.

stories.title: Bloc à Bleau climbing gym gets green light after months of work
stories.summary: After months of construction, the long-awaited Bloc à Bleau climbing gym has officially opened, generating strong local anticipation. The project is expected to quickly attract a dedicated following in the Seine-et-Marne region.

stories.title: Greifswald bouldering gym files for insolvency
stories.summary: The Grips-Boulderhalle in Greifswald, Germany has filed for insolvency, with operators saying 'this chapter ends here.' Reports suggest a new chapter could begin for the climbing facility under different management.

stories.title: Fatal fall at Luxembourg climbing gym prompts investigation, casts doubt on upcoming competition
stories.summary: A 37-year-old man died after falling roughly seven metres at a climbing gym in Zolver/Soleuvre, Luxembourg. The gym remained open following the incident, but the status of the upcoming Petzl Lead Open competition (12 June–4 July) is uncertain as police investigate the circumstances of the fall.

stories.title: Operator responds after fatal climbing accident in Soleuvre (Zolver)
stories.summary: A 37-year-old man died after a fall of several metres at a climbing gym in Soleuvre, Luxembourg, on Monday evening. The gym's operator has issued a statement following the incident, and prosecutors have ordered an autopsy as part of the investigation.

stories.title: New DAV climbing hall and clubhouse proposed for Zeche Zollverein, Essen
stories.summary: An architecture firm has presented its competition entry for a new DAV (German Alpine Club) climbing hall with an accompanying clubhouse, to be built on the site of the former Zollverein coking plant in Essen. The proposal placed second in an invitational design competition for the historic industrial site.

stories.title: Blocabrac climbing gym in Saint-Étienne celebrates 10th anniversary
stories.summary: The Blocabrac climbing gym in the Grand-Clos area of Saint-Étienne, France, marked its tenth anniversary with a festive evening event including a competition. The gym welcomes beginners and experienced climbers daily and has become an established part of the local climbing scene.

stories.title: Hamburg shopping centre transforms into climbing wall again
stories.summary: The popular free climbing wall has returned to Hamburg's Europa Passage shopping centre in the city centre. The article offers tips on who should visit and when, as the attraction draws crowds during its limited run.

stories.title: Climbing trade show bars outlet Vertige Media as 'too activist'
stories.summary: France's Salon de l'escalade climbing trade show has denied accreditation to independent outlet Vertige Media, deeming its coverage too politically engaged. The move has sparked debate about press freedom and editorial independence at industry trade events.

stories.title: Town of Huy, Belgium to launch tender for new climbing gym
stories.summary: The Belgian town of Huy plans to issue a call for proposals to build a climbing wall behind its existing sports hall, after the town went several years without a climbing facility. The move comes as climbing's popularity grows following its Olympic recognition.

stories.title: New 4,000m² multipurpose climbing hall Monoliet under construction in Roeselare
stories.summary: A new climbing facility called Monoliet is being built on Armoedestraat in Roeselare, Belgium, spanning 4,000 square metres. The venue is designed as a multipurpose space combining climbing with other activities.

stories.title: Connected Climbing Wall Opens New Routes for Climbers in Louvain-la-Neuve
stories.summary: The Blocry sports complex in Louvain-la-Neuve, Belgium has installed a Kilter Board, an adjustable connected climbing wall, in its climbing hall. The addition is expected to expand training options for local climbers.

stories.title: Paris-Region Churches Reborn as Bars and Climbing Gyms
stories.summary: Several deconsecrated churches in the Île-de-France region are being converted into unconventional venues, including a 13-metre-high climbing gym and a cosy bar. The trend reflects a wider reuse of disused religious buildings for leisure purposes in 2026.

stories.title: New Alpine Centre Climbing Gym Opens in Schorndorf, Germany
stories.summary: A new climbing facility, the Schorndorf Alpinzentrum, has opened in Germany featuring a bouldering area, a bistro, and a sustainability-focused design. The centre aims to serve as a hub for both climbing and alpine sports enthusiasts.

stories.title: Fatal Accident at Luxembourg Climbing Gym Sparks Safety Debate
stories.summary: A 37-year-old man died following a fall at a climbing gym in Zolver, Luxembourg, prompting questions over whether indoor sport climbing carries excessive risk. The national climbing federation has responded publicly to address safety concerns raised by the incident.

stories.title: Sawiris Unveils Plans for Mega Sports Centre with Climbing Hall in Andermatt
stories.summary: Swiss investor Samih Sawiris has presented plans for a large new sports centre in Andermatt, Switzerland, which will include a climbing hall, swimming pool, spa, padel courts, bowling and an ice rink. The project, fulfilling a longstanding promise to the local community, is expected to open in 2032/33.

stories.title: Largest Bouldering Gym in Paris Opens in Seine-Saint-Denis
stories.summary: A new bouldering gym described as the largest in Paris has opened in the Seine-Saint-Denis department (93), adding to the city's growing indoor climbing scene. The opening was noted alongside coverage of a nearby free World Cup fan zone.

stories.title: Bloc'Up Aims to Reinvent the Climbing Gym Concept in Roquevaire
stories.summary: Bloc'Up, a new climbing gym in Roquevaire, France, opened on 12 June, launched by a father-son duo offering an innovative take on the climbing gym format. The gym presents a unique concept aimed at differentiating itself in the local climbing market.

stories.title: Kaufbeuren Members' Assembly Approves Construction and Operation of New Climbing Hall
stories.summary: A general members' meeting in Kaufbeuren, Germany has voted to approve the construction and operation of a new climbing hall as part of a planned facility expansion. Chairman Ralf Trinkwalder presented the concept for the expansion at the assembly.

stories.title: 'Das Flash' - New Community Climbing Gym Opens in Vienna's Brigittenau District
stories.summary: A new community-oriented climbing gym called 'das flash' has opened in a converted factory hall in Vienna's Brigittenau district. The venue aims to bring new life and activity to the local neighbourhood.
